critic_raw:
For the structured data branch specifically, the design states: 'All preprocessing transformers are designed for stability in parameter estimation by being fit on the entire data prior to splitting.' This directly contradicts the otherwise careful temporal split design. While the text and image branches use frozen pretrained models (no label-driven fitting), the structured feature normalization statistics (mean, std for continuous features, one-hot vocabulary for categoricals) are computed over all 60 months including the held-out test period. This leaks test-era distributional information into the standardization applied to training data.

The baseline is 'unimodal model using clinical notes only.' The multi-modal model adds echocardiogram images and structured data. If the multi-modal model outperforms the baseline, the gain could be from imaging, from structured data, or from both — the design cannot decompose these contributions. The hypothesis should be tested with a baseline that includes structured data to isolate the imaging contribution specifically, or with an ablation study. As stated, a significant structured data contribution would be conflated with an imaging contribution.

The hypothesis confirmation criterion is that the multi-modal model achieves higher test AUROC 'consistently across all subgroups.' This is an unusually strong requirement: requiring consistency across age groups, sex, race/ethnicity, Charlson index strata, treatment strata, and two temporal windows simultaneously means that a single subgroup showing no difference (even due to limited subgroup power) would fail the hypothesis. The design provides no power calculation for subgroup tests, making it near-certain that some subgroup will show no significant difference by chance alone, producing systematic Type II error for the primary hypothesis.

Echocardiogram image preprocessing (normalization) is computed from training images only, which is correct. However, no mention is made of echocardiogram availability rates. If only a subset of the 20,000 patients have echocardiograms, the multi-modal model is evaluated on a selected subpopulation that differs from the full cohort, potentially introducing selection bias in the comparison.
all_issues_raised: Structured data preprocessing transformers are explicitly fit on the entire dataset before splitting, leaking test-era normalization statistics into training, despite the design's otherwise correct temporal split.

The baseline is clinical notes only while the multi-modal model adds both imaging and structured data, so any performance difference cannot be attributed to imaging specifically — the contribution of structured data is confounded with the imaging contribution.

The hypothesis confirmation requires consistent multi-modal superiority across all listed subgroups simultaneously, with no power analysis for subgroup tests — this criterion guarantees Type II error as subgroup sizes shrink.

Echocardiogram availability is not discussed; if only a subset of patients have echo images, the comparison is made on a selected subpopulation that may differ systematically from the full heart failure cohort.
all_issues_adjudicated: Structured data preprocessing transformers are explicitly fit on the entire dataset before splitting, leaking test-era normalization statistics into training, despite the design's otherwise correct temporal split.

The baseline is clinical notes only while the multi-modal model adds both imaging and structured data, so any performance difference cannot be attributed to imaging specifically — the contribution of structured data is confounded with the imaging contribution.

The hypothesis confirmation requires consistent multi-modal superiority across all listed subgroups simultaneously, with no power analysis for subgroup tests — this criterion guarantees Type II error as subgroup sizes shrink.

Echocardiogram availability is not discussed; if only a subset of patients have echo images, the comparison is made on a selected subpopulation that may differ systematically from the full heart failure cohort.
